paragraphs.text: PROJECT SENATOR
SCOPE OF WORK
April 11, 2025
TAX DUE DILIGENCE SCOPE OF WORK
Our Canadian tax due diligence procedures will be performed with respect to Canadian entities in the Target structure (collectively “Canadian Target”) as listed and as described below.
InterRent Real Estate Investment Trust
InterRent Trust
InterRent Holdings Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 1 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 2 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 3 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 4 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 5 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 6 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 7 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 7A Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 8 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 9 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 10 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 11 Limited Partnership
InterRent No. 12 Limited Partnership
Silvercreek Parkway Guelph Limited Partnership
Silvercreek Parkway Guelph Limited Partnership No. 2
Park Place Equities 2000 Limited Partnership
InterRent Holdings Manager Limited Partnership

Our scope of work will generally be to the periods FY20 to FY24 and year to date FY25 (hereinafter termed as “period under investigation"). References to accounts and associated data are for these periods.

Our deliverable will be a full scope tax due diligence report on all identified and potential tax exposures and significant tax attributes expected to be acquired.  This Report will be sufficient for RWI purposes.
Due diligence procedures will be undertaken through discussions with management, and where applicable, Target’s tax advisors, and through reading limited documents as described below.

The following summarizes our estimated fees.  Our professional fees will be based on the actual time required to complete the agreed upon work at hourly rates identified for the professionals assigned to the engagement, however, would be capped as follows:
Tax due diligence		 	CAD$175,000
Tax due diligence (failed deal) 		CAD$150,000
Canadian Tax Due Diligence Procedures
Our Canadian tax due diligence procedures for the period under investigation consist of the following procedures.
General
Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about:
Overview of business operations of the Significant Target Entities and detailed description of Target’s revenue streams;
Nature of systems to ensure compliance with tax laws (in applicable jurisdictions including sales tax, and payroll).

Significant tax planning arrangements and transactions (including strategies and related transaction costs), dispositions/divestitures, financings, and reorganizations), and tax treatment thereof, including benefits obtained;
Any unclaimed deductions such as CCA or financing/share issuance costs;
Procedures regarding monitoring ACB and at-risk amounts;

Results of significant tax audits including jurisdictions subject to audit, quantum of known or proposed audit adjustments and, status of current/pending audits (income tax, sales tax, land transfer tax, and payroll tax), and compliance history in respect of same;

Historical tax positions taken including the quantum of reserves for uncertain tax positions included in the financial statements and the basis for those reserves (unclaimed deductions such as CCA, land transfer tax);
Canadian Target’s cross-border business travel, including Regulation 102 and 105 withholding obligations, if applicable;
COVID-19 government relief received (Canada Emergency Wage Subsidy), and tax compliance thereof;
Quantum of non-arm’s length transactions including basis of pricing/valuation, type of transaction and documentation available to support the transactions; and,
Results of analysis undertaken in respect of Excessive Interest and Financing Expenses Limitation (“EIFEL”) legislation.

Obtain and read correspondence from internal and external tax advisors with respect to significant tax matters, including acquisitions (such as tax due diligence reports in respect of recent acquisitions), dispositions, restructurings, and tax strategies of the Significant Target Entities, as made available to us.
Obtain and read the tax sections of the audit working paper files for FY23 and FY24.
Obtain and read summary analysis of uncertain tax positions for the last two taxation years prepared by Target.
Income tax

Obtain and read tax returns and withholding tax returns filed by the Significant Target Entities, including T2, T3, T5 T5013 and provincial equivalents, supporting workpapers, related legal entity financial statements and schedules reconciling income reported on the trusts to consolidated financial statements, as made available to us.
Obtain and read schedules of tax basis of significant assets of Target.
Obtain and read REIT qualification testing analysis prepared by Target for period under investigation and comment on same in respect of the relevant provisions of the Income Tax Act.
Obtain and read MFT qualification testing analysis prepared by Target for period under investigation and comment on same in respect of the relevant provisions of the Income Tax Act.
Obtain and read an estimate of the inherent recapture in the structure.
Obtain and read documentation supporting related party arrangements, as made available to us.
Obtain and read tax election filed by Significant Target Entities, as made available to us.
Obtain and read the adjusted cost base and at-risk amount calculations in respect of any partnerships within the structure and obtain and read of partnership agreement(s) if applicable.
Employment taxes
Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about:
Target’s payroll tax compliance status, including internal procedures related to payroll tax compliance;
Whether Target has been subject to and results of significant payroll tax audits;
Extent of use and tax treatment of individual independent or self-employed contractors, temporary workers or ‘casual workers’;
Payments to key employees that are contingent on closing of the Transaction; and

Long term incentive plan, employee unit purchase plan and special bonus programs for key employees contingent on closing and consider deductibility and significant employee taxable benefit implications of such plans.
Sales tax
Inquire with Target management and/or their tax advisors about:
Target’s indirect tax compliance status, including internal procedures related to indirect tax compliance;
Target’s indirect tax treatment of related party transactions including management and incentive fees; and

Indirect tax status of Target’s revenue, e.g., taxable/non-taxable supplies, and restrictions on recovery of input tax credits (including detailed schedule of methodology used to determine/pro-rate input tax credits);
Obtain and read related party GST/HST and QST elections.
Reporting
Based on the above sources of information, consider, and prepare a tax due diligence report including supporting analysis on potential tax exposures identified.
FINANCIAL DUE DILIGENCE SCOPE OF WORK

Unless otherwise noted, our work will concentrate on consolidated financial information for the last two fiscal years December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2024, and the latest available trailing twelve months (TTM Feb/Mar-25).

The following summarizes our estimated fees.  Our professional fees will be based on the actual time required to complete the agreed upon work at hourly rates identified for the professionals assigned to the engagement, however, would be capped as follows:
Financial due diligence		 CAD$225,000
Financial due diligence reporting 	CAD$25,000

Accounting overview
Understand and summarize the financial statement preparation process, including:

Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships (including processes, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/accounting systems);
Significant accounting policies and estimates and contemplated changes; and
Reporting methodology (structure and process) and consolidation process, period ending process and related party transactions.

Sustainability of net operating income on a consolidated basis
Summarize potential adjustments to historical Net operating income (“NOI”) on a consolidated basis by considering:
Management’s proposed adjustments to reported NOI and inquire about the nature and rationale of such adjustments and supporting documentation, if any;
The impact of any changes to senior management or team structure;
The elimination of public company costs;
Consider the pro forma impact of new units, lost units, acquired, developed, sold properties and expiring leases;

The pro forma impact consideration is contingent on readily available information from Target that summarizes its business trends such as revenue and margins by location, by community, by type, occupancy rates, lease rates, etc. for which the information has been reconciled to the Target’s financial statements and can be used to formulate the potential pro forma consideration. To the extent the information is not readily available or not transferrable to calculate a potential impact, we will discuss with Client the applicability of the impact and potential time effort to analyze accordingly.
The impact of provisions, management estimates, or adjusting entries on the reported results;
Any other unusual or non-recurring events (revenue or expenses) or transactions that may have distorted results; and
Other potential items discovered in the due diligence process.
Obtain and read an analysis of the Company’s operating expenses and inquire about
Primary components of property operating costs, property tax expenses;
Rent bad debt expense trends;
Wages, payroll taxes and benefits, by operating segment and function, if possible;
Incentive based compensation;
General and administrative expenses;
Sales and marketing and other expenses.

Balance sheet
Summarize and understand Target’s balance sheet profile:
Comment on the working capital trends, for the respective periods including unusual trends, unusual balances.

Accounts receivable: Obtain and read an analysis of the Company's accounts receivable and inquire about Rent receivable profile including aging analysis and provisions; other non-trade receivable balances.
Other assets (i.e., prepaid expenses and deposits):
Accounts payable and accrued liabilities:
Obtain and read an analysis of the Company's accounts payable and accrued expenses and inquire about:
AP aging analysis, terms of trade with major vendors, days payables outstanding and trade payables aging analysis;
Accrued expenses; and
Tenant rental deposits
If applicable, consider normalization adjustments to working capital.
Understand the nature of investment properties and joint ventures;
Analyze historical capital expenditure trends
Understand the components of indebtedness including:
Net debt items presented by management and other potential debt-like items;
Analysis of remaining liabilities surrounding contingent considerations; and
Significant lease and purchase obligations.
Operating cash flow / Funds from operations

Analyze the Company’s historical operating cash flow / FFO trends incorporating the impact of adjusted NOI trends from the quality of earnings analysis, the Company’s net working capital profile, and capital expenditures trends.

Audit work paper
Obtain and read the Company’s audit working paper files and management letters for the latest fiscal year:
Summarize audit matters, if any;
Comment on the nature and volume of audit differences (adjustments), use of accounting estimates, any changes in accounting policies or methods and their implications; and
Read analytical reviews for trends, unusual practices, internal control matters.
